content_en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Idris ash-Shafi'i (d. 204 AH) occupies a unique position in Islamic intellectual history as the founder of the Shafi'i legal school and the scholar credited with systematizing the discipline of usul al-fiqh — the principles governing legal reasoning from revelation. Born in Gaza and raised in Mecca, he studied under Imam Malik in Medina and under students of Imam Abu Hanifah in Iraq, making him perhaps the most broadly trained jurist in the history of Islamic law. His synthesis of the Hijazi hadith tradition and the Iraqi tradition of legal reasoning produced a distinctive methodology that has shaped Islamic jurisprudence to this day.

Kitab al-Umm (The Mother Book) is the most comprehensive surviving record of ash-Shafi'i's legal thought. It was compiled and transmitted primarily by his student ar-Rabi' ibn Sulayman al-Muradi, and it preserves — often in great detail — ash-Shafi'i's positions across virtually every domain of Islamic law. In this form, it functions both as a statement of Shafi'i fiqh and as a vast repository of hadith material, since ash-Shafi'i grounds his legal positions extensively in Prophetic reports, explaining which hadith he relies on and why. The hadith dimension of the work is of particular significance for the science of hadith and for understanding how ash-Shafi'i evaluated and applied Prophetic narrations.

The methodology of Kitab al-Umm reflects ash-Shafi'i's foundational jurisprudential principles: the priority of explicit Prophetic reports over analogical reasoning, the conditions for the soundness of hadith, and the resolution of apparent conflicts between reports. He frequently engages with the positions of earlier schools — particularly those of Medina and Iraq — critiquing them from the standpoint of hadith evidence and offering his own conclusions. His discussions of ijtihad, of the role of scholarly consensus, and of the scope of legal analogy run throughout the work, making it a practical illustration of the theoretical principles he laid out in his ar-Risalah.

The scope of the work is enormous: it covers purification, prayer, zakat, fasting, hajj, jihad, sales and commercial transactions, marriage, divorce, inheritance, and much more. Because ash-Shafi'i was still developing some of his positions during his time in Egypt — where he taught until his death and where ar-Rabi' studied under him — Kitab al-Umm often preserves his mature, so-called "new school" (madhhab jadid) positions that supersede the "old school" (madhhab qadim) positions he had held in Iraq. Students of the Shafi'i school rely on the new school positions, which are preserved most completely in this work.

Approaching Kitab al-Umm rewards readers on multiple levels. For students of Shafi'i fiqh, it is the foundational primary source — the words of the Imam himself, unmediated by the condensations and reorganizations of later compilers. For scholars of hadith, it is an invaluable window into how one of the greatest hadith-minded jurists of the classical period evaluated and deployed Prophetic narrations. For historians of Islamic thought, it captures a pivotal moment in the development of Islamic jurisprudence. The text requires solid Arabic and familiarity with both hadith and legal terminology, but the investment is richly rewarded.
content_id:
Imam Muhammad ibn Idris ash-Syafi'i (w. 204 H) menempati posisi unik dalam sejarah intelektual Islam sebagai pendiri mazhab hukum Syafi'i dan ulama yang dikreditkan dengan mensistematisasi disiplin usul al-fiqh — prinsip-prinsip yang mengatur penalaran hukum dari wahyu. Lahir di Gaza dan tumbuh di Mekah, beliau belajar di bawah Imam Malik di Madinah dan di bawah murid-murid Imam Abu Hanifah di Irak, menjadikannya barangkali ahli fiqih yang paling luas terlatih dalam sejarah hukum Islam. Sintesisnya atas tradisi hadits Hijaz dan tradisi penalaran hukum Irak menghasilkan metodologi yang khas yang telah membentuk yurisprudensi Islam hingga hari ini.

Kitab al-Umm (Kitab Induk) adalah catatan yang paling komprehensif yang masih ada tentang pemikiran hukum ash-Syafi'i. Karya ini dikompilasi dan ditransmisikan terutama oleh muridnya ar-Rabi' ibn Sulayman al-Muradi, dan melestarikan — sering dalam detail yang besar — posisi-posisi ash-Syafi'i di hampir setiap domain hukum Islam. Dalam bentuk ini, karya ini berfungsi baik sebagai pernyataan fiqih Syafi'i maupun sebagai gudang besar materi hadits, karena ash-Syafi'i mendasarkan posisi-posisi hukumnya secara ekstensif pada riwayat-riwayat Nabawi, menjelaskan hadits mana yang beliau andalkan dan mengapa. Dimensi hadits dari karya ini sangat penting bagi ilmu hadits dan untuk memahami bagaimana ash-Syafi'i mengevaluasi dan menerapkan periwayatan-periwayatan Nabawi.

Metodologi Kitab al-Umm mencerminkan prinsip-prinsip yurisprudensial fundamental ash-Syafi'i: prioritas riwayat-riwayat Nabawi yang eksplisit atas penalaran analogis, syarat-syarat kesahihan hadits, dan penyelesaian konflik-konflik yang tampak antara riwayat-riwayat. Beliau sering terlibat dengan posisi-posisi mazhab-mazhab sebelumnya — khususnya mazhab-mazhab Madinah dan Irak — mengkritiknya dari sudut pandang bukti hadits dan menawarkan kesimpulan-kesimpulannya sendiri. Diskusi-diskusinya tentang ijtihad, tentang peran konsensus ulama, dan tentang cakupan analogi hukum mengalir di seluruh karya, menjadikannya ilustrasi praktis dari prinsip-prinsip teoretis yang beliau uraikan dalam ar-Risalah-nya.

Cakupan karya ini sangat luas: mencakup bersuci, shalat, zakat, puasa, haji, jihad, jual beli dan transaksi komersial, pernikahan, perceraian, warisan, dan banyak lagi. Karena ash-Syafi'i masih mengembangkan beberapa posisinya selama masa tinggalnya di Mesir — di mana beliau mengajar hingga wafatnya dan di mana ar-Rabi' belajar di bawahnya — Kitab al-Umm sering melestarikan posisi-posisi matangnya, yang disebut "mazhab baru" (madhhab jadid) yang menggantikan posisi-posisi "mazhab lama" (madhhab qadim) yang beliau pegang di Irak. Para pelajar mazhab Syafi'i mengandalkan posisi-posisi mazhab baru, yang paling lengkap dilestarikan dalam karya ini.

Mendekati Kitab al-Umm memberikan imbalan kepada pembaca di berbagai tingkatan. Bagi pelajar fiqih Syafi'i, ini adalah sumber primer fundamental — kata-kata sang Imam sendiri, tanpa perantaraan kondensasi dan reorganisasi oleh kompilator-kompilator belakangan. Bagi para ulama hadits, ini adalah jendela yang sangat berharga ke dalam cara salah satu ahli fiqih berorientasi hadits terbesar dari periode klasik mengevaluasi dan menggunakan riwayat-riwayat Nabawi. Bagi para sejarawan pemikiran Islam, karya ini menangkap momen krusial dalam perkembangan yurisprudensi Islam. Teks ini memerlukan bahasa Arab yang kuat dan keakraban dengan terminologi hadits maupun hukum, namun investasinya memberikan imbalan yang sangat kaya.
